Home / Services / Duct Cleaning

Duct Cleaning

Improve your indoor air quality with professional duct cleaning services.

Over time, dust, pollen, allergens, and other contaminants can accumulate in your HVAC ductwork, impacting your indoor air quality and potentially triggering respiratory problems. Perma Services provides professional duct cleaning services to remove these contaminants and improve the air you breathe. Our experienced technicians use powerful equipment to dislodge and remove debris from your ductwork, leaving your home with cleaner, healthier air.

We understand the unique air quality challenges faced by Sacramento residents, particularly during the summer months. Our duct cleaning services are designed to address these challenges and create a healthier indoor environment for you and your family. We are familiar with the ductwork systems commonly found in Sacramento homes, from older homes in Land Park to newer construction in Elk Grove. After cleaning, we can also apply sanitizers and sealants to prevent future contamination and improve energy efficiency. Breathe easier with duct cleaning services!

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Inspection - Inspect the ductwork to assess the level of contamination.
  2. 02 Step 2: Debris Removal - Dislodge and remove debris using specialized equipment.
  3. 03 Step 3: Sanitizing (Optional) - Apply sanitizers to kill bacteria and mold.
  4. 04 Step 4: Sealing (Optional) - Seal any leaks to improve energy efficiency.

Benefits

  • Improved indoor air quality
  • Reduced allergens and dust
  • Better HVAC efficiency
  • Healthier living environment

Frequently Asked Questions

How often should I have my ducts cleaned?

We recommend having your ducts cleaned every 3-5 years, or more frequently if you have allergies or pets.

What are the signs that my ducts need cleaning?

Common signs include excessive dust, visible mold, and unexplained allergy symptoms.

How long does duct cleaning take?

The duration of duct cleaning depends on the size and complexity of your ductwork, but it typically takes 2-4 hours.

Get a Free Estimate
